INFORMATION REPORT             CD NO COUNTRY  Burma/China DATE DISTR.     19 Jan. 1952 SUBJECT   Developments in the Chinese School Controversy    NO. OF PAGES    2 25X1A      in Rangoon 1. LI Tze...tsar., one of the directors of the Teong Hhra Chinese School on Godwin Road, Rangoon, and also a leading member of the Kuomintang (Burma ) (KMT(B)), has applied to the Commissioner of Police, Rangoon, for the enforcement of section 144 Cr. P.C. (sic) on this school as he fears that a clash may occur between the pro-China Democratic League (Burma) (CDL(B)) and pro-.KMT (B) elements of the school.                           - 2.   About 20 pro-KMT(B) Chinese ladies, including Mrs. LT Tze-tsai, Mrs. KHAW Boon-tin, Mrs. LI Boon-tin, and Madam HSIA Yen, Principal of the Chung Chang Chinese School, met at
